  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Gets the name of this macro. Used as menu entry
      Specified by:
      getName in interface ProofMacro
      Overrides:
      getName in class DoWhileFinallyMacro
      Returns:
      a non-null constant string

    • getCategory

      public String getCategory()
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Gets the category of this macro. Used as name of the menu under which the macro is sorted. Return null if no submenu is to be created.
      Returns:
      a constant string, or null

    • getScriptCommandName

      public String getScriptCommandName()
      Description copied from class: AbstractProofMacro
      Gets a unique short name for this macro that can be used in proof scripts. If null is returned, the macro cannot be addressed from within scripts. By default, proof macros do not support scripts, thus null is returned.
      Specified by:
      getScriptCommandName in interface ProofMacro
      Overrides:
      getScriptCommandName in class AbstractProofMacro
      Returns:
      null if not supported, or a non-null constant string as the short name

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Gets the description of this macro. Used as tooltip.
      Specified by:
      getDescription in interface ProofMacro
      Overrides:
      getDescription in class DoWhileFinallyMacro
      Returns:
      a non-null constant string

      Gets the proof macro.
      Specified by:
      getProofMacro in class DoWhileFinallyMacro
      Returns:
      the proofMacro.

      protected ProofMacro getAltProofMacro()
      Description copied from class: DoWhileFinallyMacro
      Gets the alternative proof macro for the else-branch.

    • getCondition

      protected boolean getCondition()
      Specified by:

    • canApplyTo

      public boolean canApplyTo(Proof proof, ImmutableList<Goal> goals, PosInOccurrence posInOcc)
      Can this macro be applied on the given goals? This method should not make any changes but check if the macro can be applied or not on the given goals. This method may be called from within the GUI thread and be compatible with that fact.

      This compound macro is applicable if and only if the first macro is applicable. If there is no first macro, this is not applicable.

      Specified by:
      canApplyTo in interface ProofMacro
      Overrides:
      canApplyTo in class DoWhileFinallyMacro
      Parameters:
      proof - the current Proof (not null)
      goals - the goals (not null)
      posInOcc - the position in occurrence (may be null)
      Returns:
      true, if the macro is allowed to be applied
